summary: AMD CEO Lisa Su opened Computex 2024 by positioning AI as AMD's top priority and emphasizing an end-to-end AI compute strategy across cloud, data centers, embedded devices and PCs. AMD introduced the Zen 5 CPU core, claiming major gains in bandwidth, AI performance and IPC, and launched Ryzen 9000 desktop processors led by the Ryzen 9 9950X. AMD also announced third-generation Ryzen AI mobile processors, codenamed Strix, combining Zen 5 CPU cores, RDNA 3.5 graphics and an XDNA 2 NPU with 50 TOPS performance for Copilot+ PCs. Microsoft joined the keynote to discuss its collaboration with AMD on AI PCs and the Copilot+ PC category.

key_numbers.metric: Ryzen 9 9950X Blender performance uplift versus competition
XDNA 2 multitasking performance versus prior generation
XDNA 2 energy efficiency versus prior generation on Gen AI workloads
Zen 5 data bandwidth between cache and floating point unit versus Zen 4
Zen 5 AI performance with full AVX-512 throughput versus Zen 4

summary: ASUS opened its Computex 2024 launch event by framing its 35th anniversary around responsible innovation and a new AI strategy called “Ubiquitous AI, Incredible Possibilities.” Co-CEO Samson Hu described ASUS’s AI strategy across public cloud, private cloud, connected edge, and personal client devices, with AI PCs positioned as a major new interface shift comparable to the mouse and GUI. Microsoft highlighted Copilot+ PCs, ASUS’s Vivobook S15 launch with Qualcomm Snapdragon X Elite, and requirements such as at least 40 TOPS of NPU performance, while saying 50 million NPU-enabled PCs are expected to ship this year. ASUS also emphasized partnerships with Microsoft, AMD, Intel, NVIDIA, and Qualcomm to deliver AI silicon and AI applications across productivity, creativity, gaming, commercial, and edge use cases.

key_numbers.metric: Minimum NPU performance for Copilot+ PCs
ASUS Vivobook S15 Copilot+ PC launch date
ASUS Copilot+ PC shipment start date

summary: Jerry Kao argued that Taiwan's ICT industry should move beyond hardware manufacturing and focus on commercial AI applications, which he described as 'must have' and potentially make-or-break for companies. He contrasted consumer AI features, such as generating pictures on phones, with enterprise AI use cases that can create sustainable competitive advantage and also drive hardware revenue. Kao was bullish on AI PCs, saying COMPUTEX 2024 marks the 'year zero' for AI PCs and comparing AI's role in PCs to earlier growth catalysts such as the internet and Wi-Fi. He said AI PCs will require upgrades in memory, microphones, cameras, and thermal performance, but that the ultimate upside depends on the emergence of killer applications.

key_numbers.metric: Major historical PC growth catalysts cited

summary: Rene Haas described Arm as the world's most pervasive compute architecture, citing over 190 billion Arm-based chips shipped and more than 7 billion shipped in a recent quarter. The keynote introduced Armv9 as the next decade architecture, emphasizing confidential computing, memory tagging extensions, security, and AI/ML workloads across endpoints, networks, cloud, automotive, IoT, and infrastructure. Arm positioned specialized processing and its Total Compute strategy as necessary responses to 5G, AI, and the end of Moore's Law, highlighting new Cortex CPUs, Mali GPUs, and Neoverse platforms. Haas also discussed infrastructure momentum from AWS Graviton2, Alibaba, Tencent, Oracle, NVIDIA Grace, Marvell Octeon GPU acceleration, and an NVIDIA Arm HPC developer kit.

key_numbers.metric: Arm chips shipped annually by ecosystem
Healthcare executives accelerating or expanding AI deployment timelines

Ampere Altra CPU frequency in NVIDIA Arm HPC developer kit

summary: Delta Research Center Director General Dr. Tucker Chiu discussed how the AI revolution, accelerated by ChatGPT and large language models, is driving rapid demand for high-performance AI accelerators and modern AI data centers. He emphasized that increasingly power-hungry accelerators such as NVIDIA A100, H100, B100, and GB200 require more efficient power delivery networks, including vertical power delivery for the last stage to the chip. Delta positioned itself as a key supplier of non-IT data center infrastructure, including power delivery and liquid cooling solutions across air-to-liquid, liquid-to-liquid, and immersion cooling. The keynote also highlighted Delta's use of AI beyond data centers, including repurposing Wi-Fi from a communications technology into a sensing and positioning technology.

key_numbers.metric: Required current for example AI accelerator

summary: GIGABYTE presented AI TOP, a desktop/local AI training solution aimed at small and medium-sized businesses, researchers, students, and creators who want to fine-tune open-source LLMs without relying on public cloud infrastructure. The company argued that AI workloads will shift from public-cloud general AI toward customized private-cloud and local AI use starting in 2025, driven by cost, privacy, and data-security concerns. AI TOP combines AI TOP Utility software, AI TOP Tutor support, and purpose-built PC hardware including TRX50 and W790 motherboards, RTX 4070 Ti SUPER AI TOP GPUs, AMD Radeon Pro W7900/W7800 AI TOP cards, SSDs, and a 1600W PSU. GIGABYTE highlighted configurations ranging from $6,499 to $35,999, with stated support for fine-tuning models from 8B to 70B ideally and up to 236B or larger on the highest-end setup.

summary: Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger presented Intel's Computex 2024 keynote around the theme of enabling 'AI everywhere' across data centers, edge, PCs and foundry services. He launched Xeon 6 with E-cores, positioning it as a major data-center upgrade with higher core density, better performance per watt and rack consolidation versus older Xeon generations. Intel emphasized its installed base of 130 million Xeons, the role of Xeon plus RAG for enterprise AI using on-prem data, and Gaudi accelerators as open, lower-TCO alternatives for generative AI training and inference. The keynote also highlighted Intel Foundry as a 'system foundry for the AI era' and framed AI as a semiconductor demand driver toward a $1 trillion industry by the end of the decade.

key_numbers.metric: 2nd Gen Xeon media transcode demo throughput
2nd Gen Xeon media transcode frame rate
Energy savings for one 200-rack data center
eBay performance per watt improvement over competitive solutions
eBay performance per watt improvement over 3rd Gen installed base

summary: MediaTek CEO Dr. Rick Tsai framed the company’s COMPUTEX keynote around “intelligence everywhere” and the power of ubiquitous AI. He argued that AI will become embedded across daily life, spanning smartphones, smart homes, TVs, PCs, cars, enterprise environments, and cloud infrastructure. Tsai emphasized MediaTek’s role in enabling AI at the edge and in the cloud through what he called hybrid computing. The keynote positioned MediaTek as a major participant in making AI available broadly across consumer and industrial devices.

NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang argued that the computing industry is entering a major platform shift driven by accelerated computing and generative AI. He said CPU performance scaling has slowed while computing demand continues to grow exponentially, creating “computation inflation” in data centers. Huang positioned CUDA, GPUs, and NVIDIA’s domain-specific software libraries as the solution, claiming accelerated computing can deliver large performance, cost, and energy savings. He highlighted applications across AI, data processing, computational lithography, telecom, gene sequencing, combinatorial optimization, and quantum simulation, including TSMC’s use of NVIDIA’s cuLitho and Google Cloud’s integration of cuDF for pandas acceleration.

key_numbers.metric: Illustrative power increase for accelerated computing
Illustrative cost increase for accelerated computing
Illustrative GPU addition to data center

summary: TH Tung argued that AI will not be a standalone product but a broad technology trend embedded across hardware, software, services, and every layer of the ICT supply chain. He said Taiwan has strengths in chipmaking, server design, and server modules, but must also fine-tune AI models for local language, accent, and use-case needs. He highlighted edge AI on smartphones and PCs as important for privacy and productivity, while citing medical, biology, security, and astronomy examples where AI can process large datasets effectively. He also emphasized that AI and Taiwan’s industrial structure will require substantial electricity, arguing for a more economical and efficient energy mix to manage future costs.

summary: Qualcomm CEO Cristiano Amon presented Snapdragon X Elite and Snapdragon X Plus as the platforms enabling Microsoft Copilot+ PCs and described the moment as a major transition for the Windows PC ecosystem. He argued that on-device AI, enabled by Qualcomm's NPU, will redefine PC experiences across productivity, creativity, communications, entertainment, personalization, privacy, and battery life. Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella appeared by video, saying Copilot+ PCs are the fastest and most intelligent Windows PCs ever built and highlighting Snapdragon X series' 45 TOPS NPU. Qualcomm emphasized broad OEM support, citing 22 Copilot+ PCs announced from Acer, Asus, Dell, HP, Lenovo, Microsoft, and Samsung, and said Snapdragon X will expand across all PC form factors.

key_numbers.metric: Copilot+ PCs announced in first wave powered by Snapdragon X Elite and Snapdragon X Plus
Copilot+ PCs available for pre-order powered by Snapdragon X series
Snapdragon X series laptop NPU performance cited by Satya Nadella

summary: Supermicro CEO Charles Liang said AI infrastructure demand is entering a phase of fast growth and massive adoption, with Supermicro positioning itself as a leading provider of optimized GPU systems for major AI factories. The keynote emphasized direct liquid cooling as a mainstream data center solution that can lower capital costs, reduce operating expenses, cut power requirements, and reduce CO2 emissions versus traditional air cooling. Liang claimed Supermicro is already shipping liquid-cooled rack solutions in volume and targeting rapid industry adoption from less than 1% today to 15% of new data centers within 12 months and up to 30% in following years. The company also highlighted new Intel Xeon 6 and AMD Gen 5 platforms and its long-standing partnership with Nvidia for AI-optimized systems.

key_numbers.metric: H100 GPUs required for cited GPT-4-sized LLM data center
HGX systems required for cited GPT-4-sized LLM data center

Power requirement for liquid-cooled LLM data center
Supermicro DLC data center scale delivery time
Target share of global new data center development using Supermicro DLC

key_numbers.period: following years / next one to two years

summary: ASUS co-CEO Sam Hu argued that explosive growth in LLMs, inference, agentic AI and future physical AI/AGI will drive sustained demand for AI servers and AI data centers. He described cloud AI data centers as four-layer systems spanning GPU-based infrastructure, GPU resource-management software, user portals/AI platforms, and deployment/operations services. Hu highlighted operational challenges including faster GPU generation cycles, heterogeneous accelerators from Nvidia/AMD/Intel/CSP TPUs, x86 versus Arm CPU architectures, and varied workload models such as VM, bare metal and containers. He also emphasized local AI data centers for enterprises and governments, citing privacy, domain-specific fine-tuning, small language models and lower-complexity hardware as drivers of future growth.

key_numbers.metric: Government/enterprise data centers ASUS helped build
Taiwan AI data center TOP500 ranking
Taiwan AI data center Green500 ranking
Local AI data center market growth

summary: Advantech's embedded sector president Miller Jang discussed the evolution from isolated edge devices to cloud-connected AIoT and now to edge AI devices that can compute, decide, execute, and collaborate locally. He argued that edge AI is already being deployed across factories, healthcare, smart cities, logistics, agriculture, and robotics, with robotics becoming a major growth vector. Advantech highlighted its Autonomous Systems and Robotics unit, its building-block approach for robot solutions, and its edge AI hardware and robotic software suite supporting Intel, Nvidia, and Qualcomm SDKs. The company positioned itself as an ecosystem enabler for edge AI plus robotics through computing platforms, sensor integration, software tools, and safety certification support.

key_numbers.metric: Collaborative robots and humanoid robots growth forecast
Recent acceleration period for stronger AI and edge AI

summary: Computex 2025 organizers framed AI as the central force driving the next wave of technology innovation, with the event theme "AI Next" focused on AI and robotics, next-generation technology, and future mobility. Speakers argued that open-source AI has lowered costs, AI is gaining long-term memory, and intelligent robots will become partners in offices, factories, hospitals, and homes. Taiwan was positioned as a key hub for the AI era due to its semiconductor supply chain, hardware-software integration, and advanced computing capabilities. The conference also emphasized Computex and InnoVEX as global B2B platforms connecting technology leaders, startups, buyers, and capital markets.

key_numbers.metric: AI-related technology share of show floor

summary: Delta Electronics presented the escalating power and cooling challenges created by AI computing and AI data centers. The speaker argued that AI data center electricity demand is already comparable to Taiwan's annual power consumption and could rise sharply through 2027 and 2030. Delta described end-to-end grid-to-chip power architectures, including UPS, BBU, capacitor shelves for peak shaving, 800V DC distribution, and solid-state transformers to improve efficiency. The presentation also covered liquid-to-air and liquid-to-liquid cooling infrastructure, CDU deployment, cold plates, and vapor chambers as necessary technologies for higher-density AI racks.

summary: Foxconn Chairman Young Liu described the company’s AI strategy as centered on AI-powered manufacturing, or “Genesis,” developed in collaboration with partners including Nvidia and BCG. He said Foxconn is building a future factory model that starts with an Omniverse digital twin, then an AI factory layer, and finally the physical factory, enabling optimization and model training before real-world deployment. The keynote emphasized that generative AI can handle roughly 80% of manufacturing technician work, while skilled humans remain necessary for the most complex 20%. Liu also discussed Foxconn’s smart EV platform, a Mitsubishi-related EV reference design announcement, and a smart city platform intended to connect transportation, hospitals, schools, buildings, governments, enterprises and citizens through shared data and knowledge.

summary: Gigabyte presented its Computex 2025 product lineup focused on the shift of AI training and inference from cloud to edge devices. The company introduced the AORUS RTX 5090 AI Box, described as the world's fastest eGPU and positioned for gaming, creator workflows, and local AI model deployment. Gigabyte also unveiled the Radeon AI Pro R9700 AI TOP 32G, emphasizing AMD ROCm support, multi-GPU AI workloads, and advanced cooling for sustained performance. The presentation further highlighted AI TOP systems powered by NVIDIA DGX Spark / GB10 Grace Blackwell, AI TOP Utility 4.0, model conversion, local inference, and edge deployment for large language and multimodal AI models.

summary: MediaTek CEO Rick Tsai framed the company’s strategy around “AI for everyone from the edge and to the cloud,” arguing that MediaTek is one of the few companies able to deliver AI across mobile, IoT, automotive, Chromebook, supercomputer and ASIC/custom silicon markets. He reviewed MediaTek’s 28-year history across optical drives, TVs, smartphones, connectivity, computing and automotive, claiming the company has shipped more than 20 billion chips/devices. Tsai highlighted ongoing work in flagship mobile SoCs, 224G SerDes, 2.5D/3D packaging with TSMC, AI accelerators, RedCap 5G modems, satellite communications and future 6G with AI assistance. He also disclosed that MediaTek is moving to 2nm and expects to tape out its first 2nm high-volume chip in September 2025.

key_numbers.metric: System power reduction for gaming users
System power reduction for regular users

summary: Jensen Huang framed NVIDIA as evolving from a chip company into an essential AI infrastructure company, arguing that AI factories will become a new global infrastructure layer comparable to electricity and the internet. He described the modern AI data center as a single computing unit producing valuable output in the form of tokens, with industry opportunity expanding from hundreds of millions to trillions of dollars. Huang emphasized NVIDIA's CUDA and CUDA-X software libraries as the core differentiator behind accelerated computing, spanning graphics, AI, radio signal processing, genomics, medical imaging, weather, quantum, and deep learning. He also showcased real-time ray-traced graphics powered by the new GeForce RTX 5060 and discussed DLSS neural rendering, where AI generates most pixels.

key_numbers.metric: AI factory and AI infrastructure opportunity

summary: Qualcomm CEO Cristiano Amon used the Computex 2025 keynote to frame 2025 as Qualcomm's 40th anniversary and emphasize the company's reinvention beyond mobile into PCs, automotive, edge AI and intelligent devices. The presentation highlighted the first year of Snapdragon X Elite and X Plus in Windows Copilot+ PCs, claiming strong progress in native app availability, AI features, gaming compatibility and Microsoft partnership. Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella appeared by video, describing Copilot+ PCs with Snapdragon silicon as among the fastest and most power-efficient Windows PCs and noting deployments across thousands of organizations. Qualcomm also announced further gaming ecosystem progress, including Fortnite coming to Snapdragon and collaboration with Epic Games on Easy Anti-Cheat for Windows on Snapdragon. Asus joined on stage to promote its Snapdragon-powered Zenbook A14, described as the world's lightest 14-inch Copilot+ PC with up to 32 hours of battery life.

key_numbers.metric: First wave of Snapdragon Copilot+ PCs
Top global apps available on Snapdragon
Games running and optimized on Snapdragon

summary: Supermicro CEO Charles Liang presented the company’s COMPUTEX 2025 keynote, emphasizing Supermicro’s history of rapid time-to-market in CPU, GPU, and AI server systems and its role in large AI data center deployments. He said Supermicro revenue grew about fourfold over the prior three years and expects strong growth to continue over the next three years due to optimized solutions and market share expansion. The keynote introduced Supermicro’s DLC2 direct liquid cooling solution, claiming up to 40% savings in power and water consumption, up to 20% lower capex and opex, thermal capture up to 98%, and data center noise as low as 50 dB. Liang also introduced a broader Data Center Building Block Solution intended to accelerate AI data center deployment from years to quarters or months, supported by rack-scale systems, storage, switching, CDU, power, battery backup, networking design, deployment, cabling, and management services.

key_numbers.metric: share of global data center deployment
power savings from prior DLC solution
DLC2 power and water consumption savings

summary: Wiwynn Chair and Chief Strategy Officer Emily Hong argued that Taiwan remains central to the global ICT and AI infrastructure supply chain, producing over 92% of global servers and holding major shares in advanced semiconductor manufacturing. She said AI server demand and data center capex remain strong despite recent concerns, with 2024 marking an inflection point for AI servers as a share of total server revenue. The talk emphasized that rising model complexity is driving exponential increases in compute demand, chip power consumption, and cooling requirements, creating opportunities in cold plates, immersion cooling, and future package-level thermal solutions. Hong also highlighted the emergence of new AI infrastructure buyers beyond traditional hyperscalers, including sovereign AI projects, model providers, AI cloud startups such as Lambda, and regional data center hubs such as Johor, Malaysia.

summary: ASUS ROG marked its 20th anniversary at Computex 2026, recounting the brand's origins in gaming motherboards and its expansion into a full gaming ecosystem spanning components, laptops, desktops, displays and peripherals. Management emphasized AI as the next major frontier for ROG, describing AI-driven hardware and future agentic AI gaming experiences with adaptive NPCs and creator tools. Partner messages from AMD, Intel, Microsoft and Nvidia highlighted long-running collaboration around gaming platforms, CPUs, GPUs, Windows and graphics technology. The event introduced multiple ROG Edition 20 products, including lifestyle gear, peripherals, collectibles and the ROG NUC 16 Edition 20 mini PC featuring an Nvidia RTX 5090 laptop GPU.

Arm CEO Rene Haas used the COMPUTEX keynote to emphasize Taiwan’s central role in Arm’s history and supply chain, claiming about 250 billion Arm-based chips have been built in Taiwan and that 100% of Arm server CPUs are built there. He argued that agentic AI workloads are causing CPU demand to accelerate because agents require CPUs to manage, orchestrate, and deliver tokens generated by GPUs and other accelerators. Arm reiterated its view that future AI systems may need roughly 4x or more CPU cores in the same power envelope and said the CPU TAM could exceed prior expectations of $100-$120 billion over five years. The company promoted its Arm AGI CPU, now in production with TSMC, highlighting rack-level performance-per-watt advantages versus comparable x86 systems and naming customers/partners including Meta, OpenAI, Oracle, ByteDance, SAP, Cerebras, SK Telecom, and others.

summary: Computex 2026 opened with organizers emphasizing the theme "AI together," describing AI as moving from cloud-based tools into real-world, embodied systems across robotics, mobility, healthcare, manufacturing, retail, transportation, and urban governance. TAITRA Chairman James Huang said this is the largest Computex in the event's history and highlighted Taiwan's role as an indispensable AI hub, with Nvidia hosting GTC Taipei and major chip CEOs from Qualcomm, Marvell, Intel, and NXP participating. TCA Chairman Jason Chen said global AI competition is shifting from models and computing power toward complete system capacity, including chips, servers, PCs, connectivity, storage, thermal management, energy efficiency, governance, and applications. The event will feature over 1,500 exhibitors, 6,000 booths, nearly 500 startups from 23 countries, a dedicated robotics pavilion, and forums spanning the full AI stack.

Gigabyte marked its 40th anniversary at Computex 2026 and positioned itself as an AI company spanning cloud-to-client hardware and software, while focusing this event on consumer products. The company introduced new high-end motherboards, including Intel Z890 refresh and AMD X870 AORUS Infinity models, emphasizing memory speed, latency, power delivery, and advanced cooling designs. It also launched or previewed AIO coolers, a compact 1600W AI-focused PSU, MicroATX cases, peripherals, RTX 50-series AORUS Infinity GPUs, and external GPU AI Box products. The tone was highly promotional, centered on setting performance records and enabling local AI, gaming, creator, and enthusiast PC workloads.

key_numbers.metric: AI gyroid cooling surface area improvement
AORUS P1600 AI TOP power output
AORUS C510 Glass Infinity case volume
RTX 5090 Infinity airflow improvement vs traditional hollow-out backplate
RTX 5090 Infinity airflow improvement vs sealed backplate

summary: Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan framed Intel as an engineering-led company refocusing on execution, partnerships, and core compute ecosystems across PCs, edge/physical AI, data centers, and future intelligent systems. Intel said its 18A process is at full scale and that Core Ultra Series 3, its first 18A product, is shipping across more than 300 designs, with Core Series 3 already reaching 70-plus designs after an April introduction. The company highlighted ARC G3 for handheld gaming, claiming over 40% faster performance versus competition and half the power at the same performance, with devices available later in the month. Intel also emphasized edge and physical AI opportunities, citing over 130 edge designs, more than 4,000 edge ecosystem partners, and a projected $25 trillion physical AI market by 2050. Perplexity CEO Aravind Srinivas joined to discuss hybrid agentic inference on Intel Core Ultra Series 3 GPUs, positioning local AI as important for privacy, cost, performance, and token value per watt.

key_numbers.metric: ARC G3 handheld gaming performance versus competition
ARC G3 power at same performance
AAA game resolution on ARC G3 devices
AAA game frame rate on ARC G3 devices
AI models used by Perplexity Computer

summary: Marvell Chairman and CEO Matt Murphy argued that AI infrastructure performance is increasingly defined not only by processors, process nodes, or memory, but by connectivity across tens of thousands and eventually millions of processors. He said the next major wave of AI infrastructure innovation will come from system connectivity, especially as links transition from copper to optical, unlocking new architectures. Murphy reviewed Marvell's decade-long strategic transformation from consumer-exposed semiconductor businesses toward data infrastructure through divestitures, acquisitions, and internal R&D. He highlighted acquisitions including Cavium, Avera, Aquantia, Inphi, Innovium, Celestial AI, and XConn, and said Marvell has invested roughly $36 billion in building its data infrastructure platform.

Jensen Huang argued that agentic AI has arrived and is already creating measurable productivity gains, especially in software development, citing a near tripling of GitHub commits in early 2026. He framed tokens as profitable units of revenue and said this is driving demand for more AI factories and compute, benefiting NVIDIA's Taiwan supply-chain ecosystem. Huang described the new agentic computing model as a distributed system involving large language models, harnesses, memory, tools, CPUs, GPUs, DPUs and storage, rather than traditional applications running inside an operating system. He positioned NVIDIA's CUDA-X libraries as tools that agents will increasingly use and introduced Vera Rubin as an end-to-end system designed for this heterogeneous, secure, distributed AI workload.

key_numbers.metric: Global industries impacted by software developers

summary: NXP CEO Rafael Sotomayor argued that physical AI and robotics require reflex-like intelligence at the edge rather than relying solely on centralized cloud or large reasoning models. He used human biology, especially the cerebrum, cerebellum, and spinal cord, to explain why ultra-low-latency, distributed, energy-efficient control is necessary for drones, software-defined vehicles, and humanoid robots. NXP framed its 'neural axis architecture' as a three-layer model consisting of reasoning, coordination, and reflex layers, with local processing placed near sensors, motors, joints, and actuators. The keynote highlighted NXP's leadership in automotive software-defined vehicle architectures through its S32N 5nm central compute family and S32K family for zonal/reflex functions.

key_numbers.metric: Daily tasks done without conscious thought

summary: Computex 2026 opened under the theme “AI together,” emphasizing AI’s shift from cloud-based tools to real-world deployment across devices, robotics, mobility, manufacturing, healthcare, retail, transportation, and urban governance. Organizers framed Taiwan and Computex as central to the global AI ecosystem, citing record scale, a new robotics pavilion, major forums, and participation from global technology leaders. Qualcomm CEO Cristiano Amon began the opening keynote by thanking Taiwan partners, especially TSMC, and said his main objective was to explain how AI will evolve across edge devices rather than focus on data centers. He argued that AI is becoming a new form of computing that will change the human-computer interface and the architecture of personal computing devices, with this transition starting to become real in 2026.

Supermicro CEO Charles Liang argued that AI infrastructure demand is accelerating and that customers' key metric is reducing time to market, time to deploy, and time to online. He emphasized Supermicro's modular building-block architecture, pre-tested rack-scale systems, and liquid-cooling/power solutions as ways to shorten deployment cycles and improve performance, efficiency, and cost. Liang said Supermicro has shipped more than 8,000 racks over the last nine months/three quarters, is a major provider of Nvidia Blackwell GB300/B300 HGX solutions, and is preparing Vera Rubin NVL72 and Rubin HGX platforms for sampling and production. He also highlighted rapid manufacturing expansion, including 1.4 million square feet added in San Jose and a global footprint now at about 6 million square feet, while suggesting revenue could reach $40 billion this year and exceed that next year.

key_numbers.metric: DLC AI data center build time
Recent gigawatt data center completion time

key_numbers.period: last nine months / last three quarters

key_numbers.metric: Manufacturing capacity added in San Jose
